summarization.a: A process flow diagram illustrating the sequence of operations and gas flow rates during two half-cycles (A and B) in a plasma process.

a.question: What processing gas is used in this reactor?
a.answer: a	Argon is used as processing gas, this is usually done as it is inert.

a.question: Why is the ICP not turned on the moment SF6 is in the reactor chamber?
a.answer: a	The gas mixture in the reactor chamber first has to stabilize, so there is a wait step before the plasma is turned on.

a.question: Why is TMA not dosed continuously?
a.answer: a	TMA is usually dosed in a vapor drawn fashion, this method is not suited to continuous dosing as this will reduce the controllability.

a.question: How are the two half-cycles seperated?
a.answer: a	The two half-cycles are seperated by purging steps, where high flows of argon are used to flush the reactor chamber.
